title: Biological Signal Bodies
query: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT BodyName) AS bodies FROM FSSBodySignals WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate AND Type_Localised LIKE '%Biological%'
detailQuery: SELECT BodyName AS label, SUM(Count) AS value FROM FSSBodySignals WHERE Type_Localised LIKE '%Biological%' AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY BodyName ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {bodies} planet(s) with biological signals identified during the report period. This chart shows which worlds you found most inhabited — high signal counts can also indicate Stratum or multi-genus worlds where a single visit yields multiple paycheques.
details.help: FSSBodySignals with Type_Localised containing Biological. bodies is the distinct planet count as the headline scope indicator.

tagLines: FSSBodySignal biological detections compiled. The biological signal distribution map has been updated.
The scanner doesn't lie. Blue dots mean life. You chased a lot of blue dots.
High signal counts on a single world are a gift. A multi-genus planet is practically a career decision.
